text: The rate coefficients for the reactions of the Criegee intermediate CH2OO with SO2, NO2, NO, H2O and the organic acid CH3CHO, and of the CH2I radical with NO2 have been measured as a function of pressure at 295 K. Measurements were made under pseudo-first-order conditions using flash photolysis of CH2I2-O2-N2 mixtures in the presence of excess co-reagent together with laser-induced fluorescence (LIF) spectroscopy of the HCHO product formed in the title reactions, and photoionisation mass spectrometry (PIMS) for the CH2OO + SO2 reaction. The rate coefficients for the title reactions CH2OO + SO2 and CH2OO + NO2 are independent of pressure within the ranges investigated, and are (3.42 ± 0.42) × 10(-11) cm(3) s(-1) and (1.5 ± 0.5) × 10(-12) cm(3) s(-1) for pressures between 1.5 and 450 and 25 and 300 Torr, respectively. The CH2OO + CH3CHO reaction is pressure dependent with the HCHO yield decreasing with increasing pressure, indicating secondary products. Upper limits of 2 × 10(-13) cm(3) s(-1) and 9 × 10(-17) cm(3) s(-1) are derived for the rate coefficients for the reactions of CH2OO with NO and H2O. The derived rate coefficient for the reaction of CH2OO with H2O is significantly lower than those previously reported and has a larger impact on atmospheric models than the change for the reaction of CH2OO with SO2.
